retrieved_docs.title: Six Sigma Method -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: In the define phase, problems and issues within the process are outlined from both the business and client perspectives. This may include launching a team, gathering information, planning the project, and setting goals. For example, a project may define a medical error the organization wants to address. During the measure phase, further narrowing of the project focus occurs, and baseline data are collected. In the analyze phase, data are examined to help identify the root cause of an issue and help remove inefficiencies.

retrieved_docs.content: The capillary wall is an essential barrier to plasma proteins; however, the tight junctions covering the apical portion of the intercellular gaps in the nonpigmented ciliary epithelium are the most important barrier. This design results from a high protein content in the tissue fluid. As a result, there is a decrease in the transcapillary difference in oncotic pressure and high oncotic pressure in the tissue fluid. Water tends to be moved into the processes from the posterior chamber by the action of hydrostatic and oncotic pressure differences across the ciliary epithelium. [6] Therefore, secretion is necessary for fluid to pass into the posterior chamber under normal circumstances.

retrieved_docs.title: Prosthetic Heart Valve -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The atrioventricular valves are composed of flat and non-homogenous tissue leaflets (or cusps) with the mitral valve consisting of two leaflets while the tricuspid valve is composed three leaflets. The leaflets of the atrioventricular valves extend into the ventricle via fibrous chordae tendinae (literally “heart-strings”). The chordae tendinae attach the apical aspects of the atrioventricular valves to muscular projections along the ventricular wall.  These muscular projections are called papillary muscles, and they serve as a point of anchoring stability for the atrioventricular valves.

retrieved_docs.title: Aortopulmonary Septal Defect -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: An aortopulmonary (AP) septal defect, also known as an AP window, is one of the rarest congenital heart defects accounting for less than 0.5% of forms of congenital heart disease. [1] This defect can occur in isolation or concurrently with other forms of congenital heart disease, such as a ventricular septal defect, interruption of the aortic arch, tetralogy of Fallot, and, rarely, coronary artery anomalies. [2] [3] [4] [5] [6] By definition, the AP window is a direct "side to side" connection of the ascending aorta to the main pulmonary artery but with the formation of a normal aortic valve and a right ventricular outflow tract, differentiating it from truncus arteriosus (see Video. Echocardiogram of Aortopulmonary Window). [7] Embryologically an AP window develops when there is incomplete septation of the great arteries.

Each crystal within the linear transducer is not given direct electrical stimulation. The advantage of the linear probe is a larger field of view and a higher superficial focus. Common clinical applications of this probe are within the soft tissue and more superficial structures. See Image. Ultrasound Linear Probe.

retrieved_docs.title: Common Pitfalls In The Research Process -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: When considering embarking on a medical research project, one must begin with detailed planning. Do not underestimate the amount of time a project can take, often spanning years from conception to manuscript preparation. Will you conduct a retrospective chart review, a prospective study, or a true clinical trial with randomization and blinding? Will you systematically seek out and remove sources of bias from the study design and interpretation of results? Will you ensure the study is powered properly to justify conclusions? Will you eliminate or explain any conflicts of interest occurring among your author group? Will you fall victim to the temptation of frivolous subgroup analyses, or will you stick with the original plan? Will your study have a realistic chance at publication in a journal within your specialty, or perhaps another subfield? The study results may prove the null hypothesis, a ‘negative study,’ and therefore be difficult to publish. [2] Additionally, the intervention you find beneficial may subsequently be proven unhelpful or even dangerous, leading to prudent medical reversal. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Apical Periodontitis -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Apical diseases can manifest with various clinical presentations, ranging from no obvious clinical signs or symptoms to severe destruction of the underlying bone, with or without a draining abscess. Therefore, proper identification, diagnosis, and treatment of apical diseases can prevent severe clinical signs and symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: Credentialing -- Definition/Introduction -- What is New in Credentialing?
retrieved_docs.content: Credentialing is a vital process for healthcare institutions. In simple terms, it is the process of assessing a healthcare provider's academic qualifications and clinical practice history. Credentialing is not a novel concept; it has been practiced for more than 1000 years when physicians in Persia had to demonstrate their skills and training before they were allowed to practice their art.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2267
retrieved_docs.content: Obstetrics excision is a broad surgical category that includes several specific obstetric procedures. It encompasses “Hysterotomy and termination of pregnancy,” “Induced termination of pregnancy following intra‑amniotic injection with hysterotomy,” the “Surgical treatment of missed abortion of second trimester,” the “Surgical treatment of septic abortion,” the Arabic‑language procedure “إنهاء الحمل خارج الرحم” (termination of ectopic pregnancy), and the Arabic‑language procedure “ولادة قيصرية في الجزء السفلي” (lower‑segment cesarean delivery). These entities are linked as child concepts under the umbrella of obstetrics excision, reflecting the range of operative interventions used to manage abnormal or non‑viable pregnancies and to deliver a fetus when surgical delivery is required.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2061
retrieved_docs.content: Perinatal Terminology encompasses a broad spectrum of obstetric concepts and conditions that are essential for precise communication in maternal‑fetal medicine. It includes the term “vaginální porod po císařském řezu,” which denotes a vaginal birth after a previous cesarean section, a delivery option that requires careful assessment of uterine scar integrity. The Arabic concept “انْصِمامٌ بالسَّائِلِ السَّلَوِيّ” describes fluid embolism, a rare but potentially fatal event in which embolic material enters the circulation, demanding rapid recognition and intervention. “حمل بطني” refers to an abdominal pregnancy, a form of ectopic gestation where the embryo implants within the abdominal cavity, posing significant risks to both mother and fetus. “حمل مطول” indicates a prolonged or post‑term pregnancy, a condition associated with increased perinatal morbidity that often prompts consideration of induction or delivery planning. The phrase “رحى عدارية خبيثة” identifies a malignant uterine fibroid (or malignant uterine tumor), a neoplastic growth that can complicate pregnancy by causing obstruction, bleeding, or invasion of surrounding structures. “مشيمة ملتصقة” (placenta accreta) describes abnormal adherence of the placenta to the uterine wall, a serious condition that can lead to massive hemorrhage during delivery and frequently necessitates surgical management. Finally, “ولادة مهبلية” simply denotes vaginal delivery, the physiological process of giving birth through the birth canal. Collectively, these terms illustrate the diverse range of normal and pathological scenarios captured within Perinatal Terminology, providing clinicians with a comprehensive lexical framework for diagnosis, risk stratification, and optimal patient care in the perinatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2527
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes the medical concepts of إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) and العقم (infertility) within its subset, indicating that these reproductive health conditions are recognized and standardized in the FDA’s terminology framework for devices and radiological health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2394
retrieved_docs.content: Obstetrics excision is a broad surgical category that includes a variety of procedures used to manage pregnancy‑related conditions and deliveries. Within this category, aspiration curettage of the uterus after abortion is performed to remove retained products of conception following an induced abortion. Hysterotomy and termination of pregnancy involve a surgical opening of the uterus to terminate a pregnancy, often when other methods are contraindicated. Surgical treatment of missed miscarriage of the third trimester refers to operative removal of a non‑viable fetus and placenta when a miscarriage is not expelled spontaneously in the late stage of pregnancy. Therapeutic termination of pregnancy by insertion of laminaria utilizes osmotic dilators to gradually dilate the cervix before evacuating the uterine contents. The Arabic‑language procedures “إنهاء الحمل خارج الرحم” (termination of ectopic pregnancy) and “ولادة قيصرية في الجزء السفلي” (lower‑segment cesarean delivery) are also classified under obstetrics excision, as they involve surgical removal of a gestational implantation outside the uterine cavity and delivery of a fetus through a transverse incision in the lower uterine segment, respectively. Together, these interventions illustrate how obstetrics excision encompasses essential surgical techniques for treating missed or abnormal pregnancies, performing therapeutic terminations, and delivering infants when vaginal birth is not feasible, thereby playing a critical role in comprehensive obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1497
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes the reproductive health concepts إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) and العقم (infertility) within its defined subset of terms. In this framework, إجهاض فائت is linked to ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus), reflecting the clinical relationship between a fetal death that remains in utero and the eventual delivery of a non‑viable infant. Recognizing both إجهاض فائت and العقم in the FDA terminology underscores their importance for device regulation, diagnostic classification, and the management of patients experiencing these reproductive disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1850
retrieved_docs.content: Abdominal pregnancy (حمل منتبذ) is clinically associated with laparotomy (شق البطن), because surgical entry into the abdominal cavity is frequently required for the diagnosis, evaluation, and definitive management of this rare ectopic gestation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 134
retrieved_docs.content: Obstetrics excision is a broad surgical category positioned under the parent concept “abortion (termination of pregnancy)” and encompasses multiple specific procedures that manage miscarriage and termination of pregnancy. Child procedures of obstetrics excision include aspiration curettage of the uterus after abortion, aspiration curettage of the uterus for termination of pregnancy, hysterotomy and termination of pregnancy, and induced termination of pregnancy following intra‑amniotic injection with hysterotomy, all directly linked to the Arabic term إجهاض (abortion). Additional child concepts are surgical treatment of miscarriage of any trimester, surgical treatment of missed abortion of the first, second, and third trimesters, surgical treatment of septic abortion, therapeutic termination of pregnancy by insertion of laminaria, إجهاض علاجي (therapeutic abortion), إنهاء الحمل خارج الرحم (extra‑uterine pregnancy termination), and ولادة قيصرية في الجزء السفلي (lower‑segment cesarean delivery). Each of these procedures represents a particular method of achieving or managing abortion, with إجهاض serving as a broader parent term. The classification HAURGALTZEA, ERAGINDAKOA is defined as an inverse‑isa of إجهاض, representing a specific type or synonym of abortion; it maps directly to إجهاض علاجي and is considered synonymous with الإجهاض المحرض (induced abortion). The general concept of abortion (termination of pregnancy) also classifies الإجهاض المحرض, linking the broader term to the induced‑abortion category used by HAURGALTZEA, ERAGINDAKOA. Together, these relationships illustrate how various clinical procedures and terminologies—ranging from surgical management of missed or septic abortions to therapeutic termination with laminaria—are organized under the overarching concepts of إجهاض, إجهاض علاجي, and الإجهاض المحرض in both Arabic and English medical nomenclature.

retrieved_docs.content: Previous c‑section and maternal care for other types of scar from a prior cesarean delivery are both classified under the term “عملية قيصرية سابقة,” which denotes a previous cesarean operation. This classification groups the English concepts of a prior c‑section and related maternal scar‑care together with the Arabic label, highlighting their shared relevance in obstetric history and maternal care planning.

The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes a wide range of reproductive‑health concepts such as preterm labor, missed abortion (إجهاض فائت), infertility (العقم), tuberculous cavity infection (عدوى في الجوف السلوي), and optional termination of pregnancy. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2846
retrieved_docs.content: The concept of an allowed qualifier is applied to the prevention and control of mitral valve stenosis, threatened abortion, and fetal distress syndrome. Preventive and control strategies for mitral valve stenosis aim to limit valve narrowing and reduce cardiac complications. In cases of threatened abortion, early identification and appropriate interventions are essential to prevent progression to miscarriage and to protect maternal and fetal health. For fetal distress syndrome, vigilant monitoring and timely therapeutic measures are crucial to manage fetal compromise and improve outcomes for both mother and child. Integrating prevention and control measures for these conditions supports better patient care and reduces the risk of adverse events.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 142
retrieved_docs.content: Uterine scar from previous surgery is classified as an abnormality of the pelvic organs and soft tissues that influences gestation and is linked to both antenatal and post‑natal care. This finding, together with complications such as partial molar pregnancy (mola hydatidosa partialis) and miscarriage with genital‑pelvic infection (spontaneous abortion), forms the maternal‑pregnancy complication hierarchy. Obstetric interventions are organized into a multi‑level taxonomy. At the top, “Delivery finding” is an umbrella concept that includes mutilating procedures for delivery, abnormal delivery, breech delivery (ولادة بمجيء مقعديّ), and single‑birth deliveries performed with forceps or a vacuum extractor. retrieved_docs.content: Obstetrics excision is a broad surgical category that serves as the parent of the general concept abortion (termination of pregnancy) and encompasses a range of specific procedures linked to the Arabic term إجهاض (abortion). Child procedures include aspiration curettage of the uterus after abortion, aspiration curettage of the uterus for termination of pregnancy, hysterotomy and termination of pregnancy, and induced termination of pregnancy following intra‑amniotic injection with hysterotomy; each of these is a distinct technique for terminating a pregnancy. The category also contains defined surgical treatments of miscarriage across all trimesters—surgical treatment of miscarriage of any trimester, surgical treatment of missed abortion of the second trimester, surgical treatment of missed miscarriage of the first trimester, surgical treatment of missed miscarriage of the third trimester, and surgical treatment of septic abortion—all of which are children of Obstetrics excision and are

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 145
retrieved_docs.content: Obstetrics excision is the parent concept for a range of surgical interventions that terminate or manage pregnancy loss, including aspiration curettage of the uterus after abortion, aspiration curettage for termination of pregnancy, hysterotomy with termination of pregnancy, and induced termination following intra‑amniotic injection with hysterotomy. These procedures are classified under the Arabic term إجهاض (abortion) as narrower or child concepts. Obstetrics excision also encompasses surgical treatment of miscarriage in any trimester, surgical treatment of missed abortion in the first, second, and third trimesters, surgical treatment of septic abortion, and therapeutic termination of pregnancy by insertion of laminaria, each linked to إجهاض. Specific child nodes of إجهاض include إجهاض علاجي (therapeutic abortion), إنهاء الحمل خارج الرحم (termination of pregnancy outside the uterus), and ولادة قيصرية في الجزء السفلي (lower‑segment cesarean delivery). The entity HAURGALTZEA, ERAGINDAKOA is defined as an inverse_isa of إجهاض, representing a particular instance of induced abortion; it serves as a value for the GDC property výsledek těhotenství (pregnancy outcome), maps to إجهاض علاجي, and is related or synonymous with الإجهاض المحرض (induced abortion). The concept “Finding related to pregnancy” is an inverse_isa of výsledek těhotenství, situating it within the spectrum of pregnancy outcomes. The broader term “abortion (termination of pregnancy)” classifies الإجهاض المحرض and is a child of إنهاء الحمل (جراحي) (surgical termination of pregnancy), indicating that induced abortion is a subtype of surgical pregnancy termination. Collectively, these relationships form a structured taxonomy linking surgical abortion procedures, therapeutic methods, outcome classifications, and Arabic terminology within the overall framework of pregnancy termination and miscarriage management.
